user_query:
I’m preparing an outreach package to foreign desks and need both a clean pitch and three tailored emails that meet my tone rules. Using the provided inputs, please deliver all three outputs and a machine-readable validation report:

Inputs:
- input/pitch_draft.md (my current, too-long pitch)
- input/tone_rules.yaml (rules to enforce tone, clarity, and brevity)
- input/contacts.csv (recipients and topic hints)

Deliverables:
1) output/pitch_rewrite.md
   - Rewrite the pitch for tone, clarity, and brevity so it passes all pitch_rules in input/tone_rules.yaml:
     - Must include all required_keywords, include the neighborhood specified, preserve at least one number from the original draft, and stay within max_word_count.
     - Must not contain any banned_words and must have no exclamation marks.
   - Do not add external facts; rely on and refine what’s in the draft.

2) output/emails.csv
   - Draft one personalized outreach email per contact with columns: name,outlet,subject,body.
   - Each email must pass email_rules in input/tone_rules.yaml:
     - Subject must be ≤ subject_max_chars and must contain the exact topic_hint string from contacts.csv (case-insensitive match is fine).
     - Body must include the recipient’s name and the specified neighborhood, include at least one of required_keywords_any, avoid banned_words, have no exclamation marks, and stay within max_body_words.
   - Keep the ask clear and concise; do not paste the full pitch—write a short tailored message referencing the same story angle.

3) output/validation_report.json
   - Produce a validation report that checks the rewritten pitch and each drafted email against input/tone_rules.yaml. Use the following structure so I can re-check deterministically:
     {
       "pitch": {
         "word_count": <int>,
         "exclamation_count": <int>,
         "contains_required_keywords": {"<keyword>": true|false, ...},
         "contains_banned_words": [<list of banned words found>],
         "preserved_numbers": [<numbers from original found in rewrite>],
         "includes_neighborhood": true|false,
         "passed": true|false
       },
       "emails": {
         "rows": [
           {
             "recipient": "<name>",
             "subject_length": <int>,
             "exclamation_count": <int>,
             "contains_required_keywords_any": true|false,
             "contains_banned_words": [<list>],
             "includes_recipient_name": true|false,
             "includes_neighborhood": true|false,
             "subject_contains_topic_hint": true|false,
             "body_word_count": <int>,
             "passed": true|false
           }
         ],
         "all_passed": true|false
       }
     }
   - The report should reflect the actual contents of output/pitch_rewrite.md and output/emails.csv checked against input/tone_rules.yaml and the numbers extracted from input/pitch_draft.md.

Success criteria:
- The rewritten pitch and all three emails must pass their respective rules (validation_report.json should show passed=true for the pitch, passed=true for each email row, and all_passed=true for emails).
- All paths and file formats must match exactly as specified. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
